mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance or negative mean during periods of rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and existing home sales indicate a collapsing housing sector that drags down construction spending and household wealth, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when both series decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the industrial production index following a sharp contraction reflects renewed manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat during confirmed industrial production rebounds over a 12-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate earnings and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns show a positive correlation with CPIAUCSL growth rates over a 3-year rolling window with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 in a single month confirms robust economic expansion and supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where payroll growth surpasses 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and retail sales shows no predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts implemented while inflation remains above 210 on the CPI index signal aggressive central bank intervention to prevent recession, boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns during high-inflation rate cut periods underperform the market baseline by more than 200 basis points over 20 days.
